steps: Distance = speed \(\times\) time. Nicole walks at 2 mph for \(w\) hours, so walking distance = \(2w\) miles. She runs at 7 mph for \(r\) hours, so running distance = \(7r\) miles.
Add the two distances to get the total: \(2w + 7r = 13\).

Evaluate each choice:
  • A: \(2w + 7r = 13\) — speed times time for each activity, summing to 13 miles. This matches the equation derived above. That matches choice A — A is correct.
  • B: \(\frac{1}{2}w + \frac{1}{7}r = 13\) — uses reciprocals of speed (time per mile), not speed itself. Incorrect.
  • C: \(\frac{1}{2}w + \frac{1}{7}r = 117\) — wrong coefficients and wrong total. Incorrect.
  • D: \(2w + 7r = 117\) — correct coefficients but the problem states 13 miles, not 117. Incorrect.
